channel.title: End Grain on My Wood Gallery
channel.description: Recent content in End Grain on My Wood Gallery

item.title: Wood Grain in Cutting Boards
item.description:
Grain Faces: three sides of a board

Photo by Joel & Jasmin Førestbird on Unsplash

Face grain

Wood boards have three grain types: face, edge, and end. Face grain is the widest side of the board, Face grains offer natural wood growth-ring patterns that can either high contrasting curved arches (a.k.a. “cathedrals”), elipses, straight lines, curly or even “tiger” striped figures. In wood working, a single, wide board showing face grain is usually the prettiest presentation of the wood. Some projects benefit artisically and structurally by having very straight face grain, while other projects favor art over durability for random patterns of face grain.
